column1: # Source: BfDI orientation paper plus BDSG 2024 amendment (AI Act carve-outs
# Note: BDSG concretises GDPR opening clauses for Germany; most sections reference GDPR articles directly.

column9: Scope — BDSG §1 aligned with GDPR Art.2 material scope, extended to non-automated processing.
Video surveillance lawful basis — GDPR Art.6 opening clause.
Processing of special categories — German opening clause to Art.9.
Employee data processing — BDSG §26 is the German opening clause under GDPR Art.88.
Scoring and credit agencies — BDSG §31 concretises automated decision-making rules of Art.22.
Right of access — BDSG §34 defines restrictions to Art.15 for specific contexts (e.g. journalism).
Right to erasure — BDSG §35 defines exceptions in line with Art.17(3).
Appointment of DPO — BDSG §38 lowers GDPR Art.37 threshold (20 employees handling personal data).
Administrative fines — BDSG §43 concretises GDPR Art.83 in the German enforcement context.
Special provisions for processing in context of criminal matters — references GDPR definitions, maps via Art.4.
Processing for purposes of criminal prosecution — conditional on Art.6(1)(e).
Security of processing for criminal matters — aligned with GDPR Art.32.
